moveAnnotations.annotation: We're playing the English Opening, a flexible and strategically rich system that avoids the main theoretical lines of 1.e4. By controlling d5 and preparing to fianchetto our pawn on g2, we set up a solid, space-gaining setup while keeping our options open. This move is characteristic of the English—we're building influence over the center rather than occupying it immediately, which gives us strategic flexibility in the middlegame.
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: English Opening controls d5 and fianchettoes the pawn on g2, building central influence without immediate occupation.

moveAnnotations.annotation: Black challenges our center immediately, striking at c4 and claiming space in the middle. This aggressive response shows Black wants to dictate the tempo early and prevent us from setting up our ideal formation. The move is natural but commits Black to a concrete battle rather than a slow strategic squeeze—which is exactly what the English Opening is designed to handle.
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: Black's c5 challenges the center and commits to a concrete battle, which the English Opening handles.

moveAnnotations.annotation: We accept the pawn and open the c-file for potential use by our rook or queen later. By capturing with the c-pawn, we stay flexible about our pawn structure and maintain control of key central squares. This is the main line—we're not afraid of Black's counterplay because our pieces will have good activity and our space advantage is real.
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: Capturing with the c-pawn accepts the gambit, opens the c-file, and keeps central control with a space advantage.

moveAnnotations.annotation: Black supports the d5 pawn and prepares to challenge our center by developing pieces toward the middle and queenside. This move sets up the possibility of ...Qxd5 or ...c6, reinforcing the d5 position. Black is playing the Löhn Gambit—accepting a temporary pawn deficit to gain active piece play and central presence—which shows Black wants sharp, tactical complications rather than grinding endgames.
moveAnnotations.shortNarration: Black's d5 push gambits a pawn for active piece play and central control, aiming for sharp tactics.
